scope: core legal-institutional score for the thesis and Atlas

anchor_levels.5: partial framework or functionally limited approach
anchor_levels.7.5: advanced but incomplete or transitional framework
anchor_levels.10: operative, clear, and relatively comprehensive framework

criteria_notes.regulatory_clarity: quality and operative certainty of the legal framework
criteria_notes.private_innovation_openness: room for regulated private innovation and market entry
criteria_notes.investor_user_protection: safeguards for users, custody, conduct and disclosure
criteria_notes.institutional_coherence: clarity of mandates and coordination among competent authorities
criteria_notes.stablecoin_regime: existence and maturity of a dedicated or functionally adequate stablecoin framework
criteria_notes.tokenization_legal_security: legal support for tokenized assets and market infrastructure
criteria_notes.supervisory_capacity: authorisation, oversight, reporting and supervisory operability
criteria_notes.state_centralization_risk: degree to which the framework avoids excessive concentration of digital monetary/control power

consistency_rules: Official in-force sources prevail over speeches, consultations and soft law.
Stablecoin scores are not inferred solely from general CASP regulation.
The European Union is treated as a single jurisdiction where the regulatory core is harmonised.
The core score excludes taxation and mining/hardware, which are tracked separately as supplementary analytical blocks.
Scores are read jointly with trend, summary and, where relevant, FSB implementation stage.
